epics.title: Headless nav core (OpenCPN model/ + patch series)
epics.outcome: One cohesive C++ safety core links OpenCPN model/ headless, runs Routeman/AIS/tracks/persistence, and stays the single source of truth for live nav — the foundation every other stream consumes.
epics.definitionOfDone: Engine builds green from pinned OpenCPN SHA + patch series via bootstrap.sh; headless Routeman drives active-route nav; AIS decode/CPA/TCPA; always-on track recording; navobj.db persistence survives restart; per-field source tagging never fakes position; mock-engine + stream-smoke pass on localhost and LAN. NOTE: per-fix nav MATH (BRG/DTW/DTG/XTE/ETA/TTG/VMG) is SHIPPED and streaming ~1Hz (ENGINE-3 done) — agents must NOT rebuild it; only the clean code relocation into model Routeman (ENGINE-10) remains.

tasks.title: Vendored OpenCPN + maintained patch series build (bootstrap.sh)
Reuse OpenCPN model/ as nav engine (ocpn::model link, zero GUI-wx)
Headless Routeman active-route nav + auto-advance + per-fix BRG/DTW/DTG/XTE/ETA/TTG/VMG (math SHIPPED, streaming ~1Hz)
AIS decode + multipart reassembly + CPA/TCPA/range/bearing + age-out
Always-on distance-gated track recording (ActiveTrack)
GPX + SQLite navobj persistence (InsertRoute, survives restart)
Per-field source tagging primitive (never fakes position)
Mock-engine + stream-smoke harnesses (localhost==LAN)
Merge helm-engine + helm-tiles into one binary
Finish UpdateProgress code relocation into model Routeman (math already shipped via app-side reuse — do NOT rebuild the math)
Arm's-length GPL containment interface (S-52 engine never statically linked into a distributed binary; boat-server↔thin-client boundary)

epics.title: S-52 ENC tile engine + quilting + hardening
epics.outcome: True S-52 vector ENC rendered headless to PNG tiles over HTTP, multi-cell quilted, composited under live nav — fail-loud, deterministic, byte-identical cross-process — extending to encrypted/composite formats and the clean-room relicensing path.
epics.definitionOfDone: helm-tiles serves /chart/{z}/{x}/{y}.png; multi-cell quilting with transparent NODTA; deterministic log-scale per-tile cell pick; SCAMIN/safety-contour correctness; fail-loud status codes; MallocPreScribble regression guard; ETag/304 immutable caching; depth-on-satellite compositing (all done). Open extensions: engine-side S-52 Day/Dusk/Night palette, display-category selector, chart-object query face, chart groups, S-63/oeSENC/CM93 encrypted+composite formats, and the clean-room S-52 rebuild on permissive GDAL/OGR/PROJ + MVT vector-tile path (relicensing insurance).

tasks.title: Headless S-52 vector ENC renderer (s57chart, no GL/window)
ocpn::chart-render static library + AbstractTopFrame headless seam + chart_stubs.cpp
S-52 chart-tile HTTP server (slippy tiles, immutable ETag+304)
Multi-cell quilting + deterministic log-scale cell pick (NODTA transparent)
Native-scale SCAMIN / safety-contour correctness + fail-closed on bad scale
s52plib headless determinism + MallocPreScribble regression guard + real DPmm
Depth-on-satellite compositing + depare/depcnt/soundg extraction
Engine-side S-52 Day/Dusk/Night palette switch (not raster reskin)
S-52 display-category selector (Base/Std/All/Mariner) + overzoom/SCAMIN warning
Chart-object query face (tap any S-57 object → attributes) + plain-language card
Chart groups / region sets management
S-63 / oeSENC / CM93 encrypted+composite chart formats
Clean-room S-52 rebuild on permissive GDAL/OGR/PROJ + MVT vector-tile path (relicensing insurance, gated on IP counsel)

epics.title: Shared-shell extraction — index.html partial API + style.json fragment split
epics.outcome: The #1 collision hazard de-fanged: index.html's 85KB monolith exposes a panel-registration API (per-epic partials) and style.json splits into per-domain layer fragments merged at build, so 12 wave-2/3 epics append to their OWN files instead of fighting the shell.
epics.definitionOfDone: A panel-registration / per-epic <!-- EPIC:XXX --> partial convention lets each epic register its panel/toolbar/⌘K entry via a small JS hook without editing the monolith body; style.json is split into per-domain layer fragments (helm-wx-*/helm-ais-*/helm-place-*/helm-chart-*/helm-ownship-*) merged at build with a stable layer-namespace convention; both land BEFORE wave-2 fan-out. SHELL is the singular owner of index.html and style.json structure; downstream epics own only their registered partial/fragment.

tasks.title: Panel-registration API / per-epic <!-- EPIC:XXX --> partial convention in index.html
Split style.json into per-domain layer fragments (helm-{wx,ais,place,chart,ownship}-*) merged at build
Stable ⌘K / toolbar registration hook each epic appends one entry to

epics.title: Streaming contract + secure transport — nav frame, command-plane, channels, TLS, pairing, tokens, APNs
epics.outcome: The locked WS+HTTP contract AND its transport-security boundary as one ownership domain: snapshot+delta+seq nav framing, conn.* router with ack+owner-token, staleness tiers, resilience primitives (resume/channels/backpressure), plus one TLS origin, Bonjour, TOFU pairing, bearer-token roles, and APNs — everything native clients inherit.
epics.definitionOfDone: Snapshot+delta+seq framing with keyframes; conn.* router + ack + owner-token; LIVE/LAGGING/STALE/OFFLINE staleness tiers; configurable bind localhost==LAN; engine address resolver; /health (done) + /catalog (partial); lastSeq resume, channels/subscriptions, client-chosen rate, alarm-reliability tier, HTTP/2 tile mux landed; one TLS origin (nav WS + chart + catalog/health/pair); Bonjour _helm._tcp; TOFU pairing (QR/PIN → token + cert pin, offline); bearer tokens with view-only/owner roles; APNs critical alerts + remote off-boat drag alert with local fallback. CONTRACT owns the frame DECODE + event-emitter (nav-client.js) and the auth-header/URL-resolution seam (server-endpoint.js); consumers subscribe to typed events and never parse frames.

tasks.title: WS command-plane (conn.* router + ack + owner-token)
Snapshot+delta+seq nav framing with periodic keyframe + nav-client.js decode/event-emitter
Staleness & heartbeat tiers (LIVE/LAGGING/STALE/OFFLINE) + 2s ping
Configurable bind + engine address resolver (local==remote transparency)
/health liveness endpoint
/catalog chart-cell endpoint (editions + bbox)
lastSeq resume on reconnect (delta-since instead of full snapshot)
Channels/subscriptions model + client-chosen nav rate (1–4 Hz)
bbox-culled AIS streaming (gated on channels CONTRACT-7)
Reconnect + latest-wins backpressure coalescing
Alarm-reliability tier + alarm wire schema (persist + re-send until ACK, exempt from coalescing) — singly-owned alarm frame contract
HTTP/2 (or HTTP/3) tile multiplexing
One TLS origin collapsing nav WS + chart HTTP + catalog/health/pair
Bonjour/mDNS discovery (_helm._tcp, TXT v/name/tls/fingerprint)
TOFU pairing (QR/PIN → token + cert pin, no CA, offline)
Bearer tokens + view-only/owner roles on /nav,/chart,/catalog
APNs critical alerts + remote/off-boat anchor-drag alert (local fallback)

epics.title: Connectivity — drivers, connections UI, source-priority, serial/internet sources
epics.outcome: User-managed boat-data connections: TCP-client/server/UDP drivers with persisted live status, NMEA 0183 ingest, serial NMEA (macOS), SignalK surfaced in UI, N2K, internet AIS, source-priority/multiplexer + raw NMEA monitor.
epics.definitionOfDone: TCP-client/server/UDP drivers persisted to ~/.helm/connections.json with reconnect/backoff and per-connection live status in nav frame; NMEA 0183 RMC/DPT/DBT/MWV/HDT over TCP 10110/UDP; SignalK promoted from env into the connections UI; source-priority/filtering UI + raw NMEA data-monitor view; serial/USB NMEA (macOS-only note); native N2K beyond SignalK gateway; internet AIS (MarineTraffic/AISHub/own feed) source. Registers its Connections panel via SHELL.

epics.title: Ownship cockpit — instrument bar, follow, orientation, predictor
epics.outcome: The live cockpit: instrument bar, ownship marker + track trail, follow-mode, course-up/head-up rotation, range rings, predictor ghost — the always-on underway view driven by the nav stream.
epics.definitionOfDone: Instrument bar + ownship marker + track trail render from nav stream; honesty badge wired; neutral-globe start + frame-on-first-fix done; follow-mode/center-on-ownship, course-up/head-up/north-up rotation, look-ahead+auto-zoom-to-speed, range rings, EBL/VRM, and predicted-position ghost shipped. Registers ownship panel via SHELL; owns helm-ownship-* style fragment.

epics.title: Route & waypoint editing — direct manipulation, multi-route, GPX
epics.outcome: Tap-to-drop, long-press-insert, drag-with-live-recompute, move/delete/reverse/split, multi-route list, drop-by-lat/lon or range/bearing, waypoint properties, and full GPX import/export round-trip — the single biggest functional gap closed.
epics.definitionOfDone: Create/save/activate route → navobj.db works (done); the new interactive verbs live in a dedicated web/route-edit.js (NOT in nav-source.js, which is only the 104-line SIM source) wired over the command router through CONTRACT's dispatch seam: edit/move/delete/reverse/split; multi-route list with activate-by-pick; drop waypoint by lat/lon or range/bearing; waypoint properties (arrival radius/icon/notes); GPX import/export UI round-trip; great-circle vs rhumb-line toggle; retrace-track-home.

epics.title: Weather rendering — scalar stack, particles, isobars, radar, ribbon
epics.outcome: Windy-parity weather rendered offline from our own GRIB/Open-Meteo: full scalar catalog, animated wind/current particles, isobars, radar, forecast scrubber, route-weather ribbon, with per-layer toggle/opacity — mostly-done, with Tier-2 GRIB/ensemble/PredictWind/true-wind as the open extensions.
epics.definitionOfDone: Full scalar stack (wind/gust/rain/temp/SST/cloud/pressure/CAPE/waves/swell/current), animated wind+current particles, MSLP isobars, RainViewer radar, forecast scrubber+play with data-age, route-weather ribbon, DEM depth contours, per-layer on/off+opacity — all offline-rendered and SHIPPED (WX-1..9 done); load-failure surfaced. WX is SOLE owner of cog.js (GRIB/COG render utility — TOOLS/ROUTING consume, never edit) and temporal.js (time-scrubber — BOARD has its own web/board.js). Tier-2 raw GRIB + value-encoded tile contract + ensemble GFS-vs-ECMWF + PredictWind import + true-wind are the open extensions.

epics.title: Native Apple clients + commercial — shared core compile, clients, cloud sync, packaging, appliance
epics.outcome: After the web contract is locked and boat-tested: compile the shared C++ core for Apple targets, ship a WKWebView first-proof, then native macOS/iPad/iPhone/Watch clients + CarPlay/widgets over the documented protocol — and the commercial layer riding on top (cloud sync, hosting, three-tier packaging, notarized DMG, license posture, appliance).
epics.definitionOfDone: Shared C++ nav core compiles for macOS/iPadOS/iOS (needs the protocol — not necessarily channels); WKWebView-wrapped web UI proves an iPad over Bonjour; native macOS SwiftUI/AppKit (+ serial NMEA via CONN-9), iPad SwiftUI+Metal, one-handed iPhone clients; Apple Watch anchor-watch/MOB + CarPlay/widgets/Live Activity; NWPathMonitor + iOS background anchor-watch; internal/BLE GPS input; onboarding/first-run flow; iOS companion deep-read tier; cloud route/mark + fleet sync; hosted tiling + GRIB delivery + remote watch; three-tier Free/Cloud/Appliance packaging; notarized non-App-Store DMG; BSL-1.1→Apache-2.0 license posture + GPL/GDAL boundary + attribution register; certified reference-hardware list + DC-DC UPS appliance + parallel sea-trial. Speaks only the documented JSON/WS + PNG/HTTP protocol (App-Store-clean).

tasks.title: Shared C++ nav core compiles for Apple targets (needs the core protocol/contract, NOT channels)
WKWebView-wrapped web UI as first iOS proof (zero new code)
Tauri desktop shell + packaging
Native macOS SwiftUI/AppKit client (+ serial NMEA via CONN-9)
Native iPad SwiftUI+Metal client (MapLibre Native)
One-handed iPhone client + NWPathMonitor + background anchor-watch
Apple Watch (anchor-watch/MOB/complications) + CarPlay/widgets/Live Activity
Internal/BLE GPS input on native + onboarding/first-run flow (connect-boat/build-board/download-home-waters/boat-profile)
iOS companion app deep-read tier (full dossier text/photos/links)
Cloud route/mark + fleet sync (CloudKit/own backend, excludes PredictWind imports)
Hosted chart tiling + cloud GRIB delivery + remote watch
BSL-1.1→Apache-2.0 license posture + GPL/GDAL boundary + attribution register (can start early)
Notarized macOS DMG distribution (non-App-Store, VLC-problem sidestep)
Three-tier packaging (Free/Cloud/Appliance) + plugin SDK / layer probe contract
Certified reference-hardware list + DC-DC UPS appliance + parallel sea-trial alongside OpenCPN

waves.rationale: Foundations that are largely DONE plus the two load-bearing un-blockers. ENGINE (nav core), CHART (S-52 tile engine), CONTRACT (streaming + now folded-in secure transport), and BACKEND (FastAPI companion) own engine/ C++ and contract files with near-zero web/index.html overlap, so they run concurrently. CRITICAL: SHELL is in wave 1 and BLOCKING — it extracts the index.html panel-registration API and splits style.json into per-domain fragments. Wave-2 epics depend on SHELL-1/SHELL-2 before they fan out, which is what serializes the 12-epic shell collision. Note wave 1 is NOT fully done: ENGINE-9 (merge), ENGINE-10 (UpdateProgress relocation, in-progress — math already shipped), CONTRACT-5b/6/7/9/10/12-16, and all of SHELL are open and gate downstream work.

Reference-client capabilities that consume the locked contract, live in their OWN module files, and append to the shell via SHELL's registration API — the cleanest parallelism in the project. AIS (collision.js/ais-meta.js), ALARM (alarms.js), WX (wind/field/isobars/radar/cog/temporal + pipeline), OFFLINE (pipeline + pmtiles/draw/lab harness), CONN (connections.js) barely touch each other. OWNSHIP, ROUTE (now with its own route-edit.js), and TOOLS still register panels through SHELL but serialize on shared shell edits, so they are NOT parallelSafe with each other. ROUTE-3 (direct-manipulation route editing — the flagged single biggest gap) is the highest-value start once SHELL-1 lands. BOAT-1 (under TOOLS) must land this wave — it gates wave-3 ROUTING-1/ROUTING-9.

Higher-order capabilities depending on wave-2 substrate: TIDES (helm_tides.cpp relocation), ROUTING (needs polars+GRIB+routes+tides; ROUTING-1 gated on BOAT-1 from wave 2), PLACES & AI (need BACKEND + WX + places store), BOARD (needs TOOLS persistence + instrument stream + its own board.js). PLACES, AI, and BOARD are parallelSafe (separate backend modules / own files); TIDES and ROUTING serialize on shared engine/contract files. AI-5's probe-contract enforcement + faces gate the plugin-SDK work in wave 4.

The user's explicit rule: native comes LAST, after the web contract is locked and boat-tested. NATIVE (now absorbing the commercial CLOUD concerns as the wave-4 'ship it' epic) depends on the shared-core compile + TOFU pairing + the documented protocol (NOT channels — the compile needs the protocol, not the optimization). The license-posture sub-task (NATIVE-12) can actually start early but the shippable cloud product gates on native + secure transport.

collisionRisks: web/index.html (85KB shared shell) is the #1 hazard — RESOLVED by making SHELL a wave-1 BLOCKING epic that owns index.html outright and ships a panel-registration / <!-- EPIC:XXX --> partial API (SHELL-1) + ⌘K registration hook (SHELL-3). Every wave-2/3 epic that adds UI (OWNSHIP-5, ROUTE-3, AIS-2, CONN-3, OFFLINE-3/4, TOOLS-3, AI-6) now DEPENDS on SHELL-1/SHELL-3 in its task deps, so the 12-epic shell collision is serialized through one owner and one API rather than concurrent edits to the monolith body.

web/style.json (shared map layers) — RESOLVED by SHELL-2 (wave-1 BLOCKING): style.json is split into per-domain layer fragments (helm-wx-*, helm-ais-*, helm-place-*, helm-chart-*, helm-ownship-*) merged at build. CHART/WX/AIS/OWNSHIP/PLACES/OFFLINE each own their fragment; layer-namespace convention prevents two epics editing the same JSON object. AIS-2 and OWNSHIP-5 carry explicit deps on SHELL-2.

web/server-endpoint.js is owned by CONTRACT; ROUTE touchesShared it for route.* commands. MITIGATION: CONTRACT lands a stable command-dispatch + auth-header seam (CONTRACT-1/CONTRACT-12); ROUTE-3 consumes it via web/route-edit.js without editing the resolver core. (SECURE merged into CONTRACT, so the former CONTRACT/SECURE fight over this file is gone — one owner now.)

web/nav-client.js is now explicitly OWNED by CONTRACT (frame decode + event-emitter, CONTRACT-2). OWNSHIP/ROUTE/ALARM/AIS subscribe to typed events and never parse frames themselves — no shared ownership.

engine/vendor/cli/helm_engine.cpp is owned solely by ENGINE. TIDES owns a SEPARATE translation unit engine/vendor/cli/helm_tides.cpp (TIDES-1) and only adds an include line to helm_engine.cpp (listed as touchesShared, not owns) — the nav loop is never edited by TIDES. ENGINE-9 (merge engine+tiles) lands before CONTRACT-12 (TLS origin) so the transport work targets one binary.

engine/vendor/cli/helm_server.cpp is owned by CHART (tile server); CONTRACT touchesShared it for the TLS-origin/token/pairing transport work (CONTRACT-12..16). MITIGATION: CHART owns the tile-serving render path; CONTRACT adds the TLS/auth front-door as a wrapping layer with a stable seam, not by editing CHART's render code.

backend/main.py routing table is touched by BACKEND (skeleton), PLACES (/places,/saved,/reviews,/giveback), AI (/context,/narrate,/whereto), and NATIVE (cloud sync). MITIGATION: BACKEND-1 establishes APIRouter-per-domain includes so PLACES/AI/NATIVE each own their router module (store.py/agents.py/publisher.py) and only append one include line to main.py.

web/integrations/cog.js — RESOLVED: WX is the SOLE owner (it is a GRIB/COG render utility). TOOLS and ROUTING are pure consumers — they call it, never list it in owns. (TOOLS's disclaimer concern moved to its own web/integrations/cog-disclaimer.js to avoid any name confusion.)

web/integrations/temporal.js — RESOLVED: WX is the SOLE owner (forecast time-scrubber, WX-6). BOARD's scrubber/state needs differ and live in its own web/board.js (BOARD-1) — BOARD does not touch temporal.js.

ALARM (alarms.js) and CONTRACT (alarm-reliability tier) must agree on the alarm wire schema — RESOLVED: CONTRACT-10 is the SINGLE owner of the alarm frame contract and is marked blocking; AIS-5/AIS-7, ALARM-6/7, and BOARD-4 all carry an explicit dep on CONTRACT-10 so the schema is frozen before any epic builds new alarm types onto it.

engine/vendor/cli/chart_stubs.cpp (links into the headless chart lib) is now owned by CHART (CHART-2). web/serve.py is owned by SHELL (dev static server alongside the shell). web/integrations/lab.js + web/integrations/_maplibre-shim.js (Lab loader harness) are owned by OFFLINE (OFFLINE-2). pipeline/fetch_glyphs.py + fetch_sat_tiles.py + make_pmtiles.{py,sh} are owned by OFFLINE. No file is now orphaned.
final.notes: FIXES APPLIED FROM REVIEW. P0 collisions resolved: (1) cog.js → WX sole owner; TOOLS/ROUTING are consumers and do NOT list it in owns (TOOLS's disclaimer split to cog-disclaimer.js). (2) temporal.js → WX sole owner; BOARD given its own web/board.js. (3) helm_engine.cpp → ENGINE sole owner; TIDES given engine/vendor/cli/helm_tides.cpp and only touchesShared helm_engine.cpp for one include line. P0 status: ENGINE-3 explicitly flags per-fix nav MATH is SHIPPED/streaming-1Hz (do not rebuild); ENGINE-10 retitled to 'Finish UpdateProgress code relocation (math already shipped)' and kept in-progress. ENGINE.status is 'mixed' (not 'done') since ENGINE-9/10/11 are open. AI-4 dep on BOAT-1 REMOVED and re-stated as shipped-against-a-stub (status:done is now consistent — no unbuilt deps). CONTRACT-5 split into CONTRACT-5a (/health, done) and CONTRACT-5b (/catalog, in-progress). OFFLINE-3/4 corrected from in-progress to todo with 'currently mockup — near-greenfield UI' note. P1: SHELL is a new wave-1 BLOCKING epic owning index.html + style.json + serve.py with SHELL-1/2/3 that downstream tasks depend on (shell-extraction + style-fragment-split now ENCODED as deps, not prose). Orphans assigned: chart_stubs.cpp→CHART, lab.js/_maplibre-shim.js→OFFLINE, fetch_glyphs/fetch_sat_tiles/make_pmtiles.{py,sh}→OFFLINE, serve.py→SHELL. nav-client.js moved into CONTRACT.owns. P2 right-sizing: 24→19 epics. SECURE folded into CONTRACT (CONTRACT-12..16 — same transport surface, one owner of server-endpoint.js/helm_server.cpp). CLOUD folded into NATIVE as the wave-4 'ship it' epic (NATIVE-10..15). BOARD kept as its own thin epic (own web/board.js) per parallelSafe value. P2 ordering: BOAT-1→TOOLS-7 and ROUTING-1→BOAT-1 / NATIVE-8→BOAT-1 gates surfaced in epic outcomes + wave-2 rationale; CONTRACT-8→CONTRACT-7 and AIS-8→CONTRACT-8 cross-wave block made explicit in titles; NATIVE-1 re-pinned to CONTRACT-2 (core protocol) + CONTRACT-14 (pairing) instead of channels. COVERAGE adds: S-63/oeSENC/CM93 (CHART-12), clean-room S-52 on GDAL/OGR + MVT (CHART-13), GPL containment interface (ENGINE-11) + license posture (NATIVE-12), internet AIS (CONN-10), serial/USB NMEA as first-class connectivity (CONN-9), device/BLE GPS (NATIVE-8), probe-contract enforcement bar (AI-5), onboarding/first-run (NATIVE-8), per-source attribution RENDERING + Overpass mirror (TOOLS-6), chart groups (CHART-11), plain-language object-query card (CHART-10). Three dedup notes retained: spacetime sampling primitive in ROUTING-3 vs narration/faces in AI-1/AI-5; boat profile modeled once as BOAT-1 (under TOOLS as a persisted settings model); Day/Dusk/Night as UI reskin (TOOLS-5) vs engine-side S-52 palette (CHART-8). Per ADRs the C++ engine stays ONE cohesive boat-only process (ENGINE+CHART+TIDES land in-binary, never split into network services); CONTRACT (now incl. transport security) is the load-bearing wave-1 epic alongside SHELL — lock both and wave-2 modules fan out nearly collision-free.

fixes: P0 — Fix the hard ownership collisions in `owns` arrays (the plan's whole premise is one-file-one-owner). `web/integrations/cog.js` is listed in THREE epics' owns: WX, TOOLS, and ROUTING. `web/integrations/temporal.js` is in BOTH WX and BOARD. `engine/vendor/cli/helm_engine.cpp` is in BOTH ENGINE and TIDES. Resolution: cog.js → WX owns it outright (it's a GRIB/COG render utility); remove it from TOOLS.owns and ROUTING.owns (ROUTING already says in collisionRisks 'ROUTING calls it, does not edit it' — so it must NOT be in owns). temporal.js → WX owns; BOARD must not own it (BOARD's scrubber needs are different; give BOARD its own file e.g. web/board.js). helm_engine.cpp → ENGINE owns; per the plan's own mitigation TIDES adds a separate translation unit (e.g. engine/vendor/cli/helm_tides.cpp) — put THAT in TIDES.owns, not helm_engine.cpp.

P0 — Resolve the UpdateProgress status contradiction. The inventory lists 'RoutemanGui::UpdateProgress gui→core relocation' as status DONE (FEATURE-TRACKER) AND the per-fix nav math (BRG/DTW/XTE) as DONE and wired end-to-end, yet ENGINE-10 marks the 'full gui→core relocation' as in-progress and OPENCPN-REUSE.md marks it partial. Pick one truth: the *math* is done (shipped, streaming ~1Hz) but the *clean relocation into model core* is partial. Re-title ENGINE-10 to 'Finish UpdateProgress code relocation into model Routeman (math already shipped via app-side reuse)' and keep it in-progress — but add a note so no agent thinks live nav math is unbuilt and rebuilds it.

P1 — Assign the orphan files. `engine/vendor/cli/chart_stubs.cpp` (real file, links into the headless chart lib) is in NO epic's owns — add to CHART.owns. `web/serve.py` (dev static server) and `web/integrations/lab.js` + `web/integrations/_maplibre-shim.js` (the Lab loader harness, a real DONE inventory item 'Lazy-isolated Lab integration loader') are unowned — assign serve.py + lab harness to TOOLS or OFFLINE. `pipeline/fetch_glyphs.py` and `pipeline/fetch_sat_tiles.py` and `pipeline/make_pmtiles.sh` (note: BOTH .py and .sh exist) are unowned — OFFLINE should own fetch_sat_tiles.py + make_pmtiles.sh; fetch_glyphs.py belongs to OFFLINE (offline vendored glyphs inventory item).

P1 — Lock the index.html shell-extraction as an explicit gating epic/task, not just prose in collisionRisks. The plan correctly identifies index.html as the #1 hazard and says 'MITIGATION before fanning out wave 2', but no epic OWNS that extraction and no wave-2 task depends on it. Create a wave-1 task (under TOOLS or a new SHELL epic) 'Panel-registration API / per-epic <!-- EPIC:XXX --> partials' marked blocking:true, and make OWNSHIP-5+, ROUTE-3, AIS-2, ALARM-6, WX, CONN-6, TOOLS-2 etc. depend on it. As written, 12 epics all 'touchesShared: web/index.html' with zero serialization mechanism encoded in deps — that is the chaos the review is meant to prevent.

P1 — Same for style.json: encode the layer-namespace/fragment split as a real wave-1 task with downstream deps. CHART, WX, AIS, OWNSHIP, PLACES, OFFLINE, TIDES, ROUTING, BOARD all touchesShared style.json with no owner of the split and no dep edges. Add 'Split style.json into per-domain layer fragments (helm-wx-*/helm-ais-*/helm-place-*) merged at build' as a blocking wave-1 task.

P2 — Fix wave/dep ordering issues. (a) BOAT-1 lives under TOOLS (wave 2) and depends on TOOLS-7 persistence, but ROUTING-1/ROUTING-9/AI-4 (wave 3) depend on BOAT-1 — fine — HOWEVER AI-4 (where-to-go recommender) is marked status:done while its dep BOAT-1 is in-progress and TOOLS-7 is todo: a done task cannot depend on unbuilt tasks. Either AI-4's boat-profile dependency is satisfied by a stub (say so) or AI-4 is not actually done. (b) ENGINE-9 (merge engine+tiles) depends on CHART-3, and SECURE-1 depends on ENGINE-9 — but ENGINE-9 sits in wave 1 with status todo while the rest of ENGINE is done; flag that wave-1 is not actually fully done. (c) CONTRACT-8 (bbox-culled AIS) deps CONTRACT-7 (channels, todo) and AIS-8 deps CONTRACT-8 — a wave-2 AIS task transitively blocked on a todo wave-1 contract task; acceptable but make the cross-wave block explicit.

P2 — Right-size: the plan has 24 epics, above the user's stated 12–18 target for a solo dev. Merge candidates: (a) fold SECURE into CONTRACT (both are the boat-server transport/security boundary, both touch server-endpoint.js + helm_server.cpp — they will collide and are one ownership domain). (b) fold CLOUD's license/packaging tasks (CLOUD-3/4) into a small 'commercial' concern and keep only sync/hosting as forward work, or merge CLOUD into NATIVE as the wave-4 'ship it' epic. (c) BOARD is thin (one integration file, mostly new web/board.js) and could ride under TOOLS+OWNSHIP. Target ~16-18.

P2 — CONTRACT.status is 'mixed' but labeled parallelSafe:false with touchesShared web/nav-client.js — yet nav-client.js is unowned by any epic's `owns`. CONTRACT decodes frames (its mitigation says 'CONTRACT owns the decode + event-emitter') so web/nav-client.js MUST be in CONTRACT.owns, not just touchesShared. Move it.

P3 — Minor status nits: WX is marked 'mixed' but every WX-1..WX-9 task is done and only the Tier-2/ensemble/PredictWind/true-wind extensions (WX-10..13) are open — that's correct as 'mixed' but call it 'mostly-done' so it isn't deprioritized. PLACES-2/AI-3/AI-4 done-status is consistent with HANDOFF-TESTING inventory. OFFLINE-1/-2 done is consistent. These are fine — listing only so the reviewer's pass is complete.
missingCapabilities: S-63 / oeSENC / CM93 encrypted+composite chart formats (inventory: 'S-63 encrypted ENC', 'CM93 vector chart support', 'S-63/oeSENC/CM93 encrypted chart formats' — all planned/missing) — not covered by any epic; CHART stops at S-57/S-52. Add to CHART.

Clean-room S-52 rebuild on permissive GDAL/OGR/PROJ (the relicensing 'insurance policy' / vector north-star, plus 'S-52 vector tiles (MVT) styled client-side') — a strategic engine item in the ADRs, owned by no epic. Belongs in CHART or a license/engine epic.

Arm's-length GPL containment interface + boat-server↔thin-client GPL boundary (ADR-0002/0003 architecture mandates) — referenced only obliquely via CLOUD-3 license posture; the actual containment-interface engineering work has no task. Add to CLOUD-3 or ENGINE.

Internal/device GPS input and BLE GPS input — inventory has these (some 'done' for device GPS in ROADMAP, BLE planned). NATIVE-8 covers 'Internal/BLE GPS input on native' but the web/engine device-GPS-as-source path and CONN coverage is absent. Confirm CONN or NATIVE owns it explicitly.

Serial/USB NMEA input (macOS) — only appears as a clause inside NATIVE-4 ('+ serial NMEA'); given it's an OpenCPN-parity connectivity item it arguably belongs in CONN with a macOS-only note, not buried in a native client task.

Internet AIS (MarineTraffic/AISHub/own feed) — inventory flags this 'near-universal among iOS competitors, not built'; no epic covers an internet AIS source. AIS epic only covers NMEA/SignalK-decoded targets. Add to AIS or CONN.

Probe-face contract as enforceable bar for new layers/plugins + Plugin SDK / layer probe contract — AI-5 covers the sample() faces for existing stubbed layers, and CLOUD-5 bundles 'plugin SDK / layer probe contract' as a clause, but the architectural 'every layer/plugin must implement sample() to be done' enforcement mechanism is not a discrete task anywhere.

Onboarding / first-run flow — appears only as a tail clause in NATIVE-8 ('+ onboarding first-run flow'). It's a named inventory item spanning connect-boat/build-board/download-home-waters/boat-profile; it deserves its own task and likely belongs partly in the web reference client, not solely native.

Permanent attribution RENDERING (Copernicus/OpenSeaMap ODbL share-alike/Windy/NOAA) and the Overpass self-host/mirror-at-scale obligation — TOOLS-6 covers the disclaimer + 'attribution register rendering' loosely, but the per-source ODbL share-alike + Windy clickable-logo + Overpass-mirror obligations are legal-load-bearing and under-specified.
nonDiscreteEpics: WX and BOARD both claim ownership of web/integrations/temporal.js — not independently ownable until temporal.js ownership is resolved (WX should keep it; BOARD gets its own file).
WX, TOOLS, and ROUTING all claim web/integrations/cog.js — three epics cannot independently own one file. WX must be sole owner; the other two are consumers (call, don't edit).
ENGINE and TIDES both claim engine/vendor/cli/helm_engine.cpp — TIDES editing the nav loop's translation unit breaks ENGINE's independent ownership of the safety core. TIDES needs a separate .cpp.

CONTRACT and SECURE both need web/server-endpoint.js and engine/vendor/cli/helm_server.cpp (SECURE touchesShared both, CONTRACT owns server-endpoint.js). The TLS-origin/token/pairing work is the same transport surface as the streaming contract — these two are not cleanly separable and are the strongest merge candidate.

ALARM (alarms.js) and CONTRACT (CONTRACT-10 alarm-reliability tier) co-own the alarm wire schema; AIS-5/AIS-7 and ALARM-6/7 also both create alarm types. The alarm-frame contract must be singly owned (CONTRACT-10) and frozen before ALARM/AIS build on it — currently the dep exists (AIS-5→ALARM-1, CONTRACT-10→ALARM-1) but the schema-ownership boundary is only in prose.

ROUTE owns web/nav-source.js but the file is the SIM nav source (104 lines); ROUTE's actual work (edit/move/delete over command router) lives in index.html + server-endpoint.js (touchesShared) and the engine command router. ROUTE's `owns` is mismatched to its actual work surface — it effectively can't proceed without editing shared files it doesn't own.
orderingProblems: AI-4 (where-to-go recommender) is status:done but depends on BOAT-1 (in-progress) and transitively TOOLS-7 (todo). A done task cannot have unbuilt dependencies — either the dep is wrong or the status is wrong.

Wave 1 is described as 'already largely DONE' but contains todo/in-progress blocking work: ENGINE-9 (todo, blocking for SECURE-1), CONTRACT-6/7/10 (todo, gate wave-2 AIS-8 and wave-3 SECURE), CONTRACT-5 in-progress. The shell/style.json extraction that wave 2 'needs before fanning out' is not even a task with deps. So wave 1 is NOT actually unblocked-and-done; the highest-value next work (route editing ROUTE-3, the flagged biggest gap) sits in wave 2 behind an unencoded index.html-extraction dependency.

CONTRACT-8 (bbox-culled AIS) depends on CONTRACT-7 (channels, todo) and AIS-8 depends on CONTRACT-8 — a wave-2 deliverable transitively blocked on a still-todo wave-1 contract feature, with no flag that channels must land first.

NATIVE-1 (shared core compile) depends on CONTRACT-7 (channels, todo) — native is correctly wave 4, but pinning the Apple compile on the channels feature (an optimization) rather than the core contract may be a wrong/over-tight dep; the compile needs the protocol, not necessarily channels.

No cyclic deps detected — but ROUTING-1→BOAT-1→TOOLS-7 and AI-4→BOAT-1 create a cross-wave chain (wave-3 routing/AI gated on a wave-2 TOOLS persistence task) that isn't surfaced in the wave rationales; make the BOAT-1/TOOLS-7 gate explicit so wave-3 owners know it must land in wave 2.
ownershipCollisions: web/integrations/cog.js — owned by WX, TOOLS, AND ROUTING simultaneously (verified: file exists; three epics list it in `owns`). No mitigation reconciles all three; collisionRisks even says ROUTING 'does not edit it', contradicting ROUTING.owns.
web/integrations/temporal.js — owned by both WX and BOARD (verified: file exists; both list it in `owns`). No mitigation.

engine/vendor/cli/helm_engine.cpp — owned by both ENGINE and TIDES. Mitigation prose says TIDES should use a separate translation unit, but the `owns` array still lists the shared file under both, so as encoded it's a collision.

web/server-endpoint.js — owned by CONTRACT; SECURE and ROUTE both touchesShared it. Mitigation (stable dispatch/auth seam) is described but SECURE's TLS/token header work realistically edits the resolver, not just consumes it.
backend/main.py — touchesShared by BACKEND(owns), PLACES, AI, CLOUD. APIRouter-per-domain mitigation is sound IF enforced; flagged because four streams append to one routing file.
statusErrors: ENGINE-10 / inventory 'RoutemanGui::UpdateProgress relocation': inventory marks the relocation DONE (FEATURE-TRACKER) while ENGINE-10 marks the 'full gui→core relocation' in-progress and OPENCPN-REUSE marks it partial. Internally contradictory; the per-fix nav MATH is shipped/DONE but the clean code relocation is partial — must be stated as such so no agent rebuilds shipped math.
AI-4 marked status:done but depends on BOAT-1 (in-progress) and TOOLS-7 (todo) — a done leaf with unbuilt deps is a status/dep inconsistency.

CONTRACT.status='mixed' lists CONTRACT-9 (backpressure) as in-progress and CONTRACT-5 (/health+/catalog) as in-progress, but /health is DONE per inventory ('/health liveness endpoint' done) and only /catalog is partial — CONTRACT-5 conflates a done sub-item with a partial one; split them.

OFFLINE-4 (BYO mbtiles import UI) and OFFLINE-3 (Download drawer UI) marked in-progress, but inventory consistently says these UIs are 'a mockup' (not started as real UI) — 'in-progress' overstates; they are mockup-only. Minor but worth correcting so the agent knows it's near-greenfield UI work, not finishing wiring.
critique.verdict: Solid bones, not yet safe to hand to parallel agents. The wave model and module-per-concern instinct are right, and the status rollup is mostly honest about the large DONE foundation. But the plan violates its own one-file-one-owner premise: three hard ownership collisions exist in the `owns` arrays (cog.js in 3 epics, temporal.js in 2, helm_engine.cpp in 2), several real files are orphaned (chart_stubs.cpp, serve.py, the lab.js harness, fetch_glyphs/fetch_sat_tiles), and the two genuinely load-bearing mitigations — the index.html shell-extraction and the style.json fragment-split — exist only as prose with zero task/dep encoding, so 12 epics nominally collide on the shell with nothing serializing them. There's one status self-contradiction (UpdateProgress done vs in-progress) and one impossible done-with-unbuilt-deps (AI-4). Coverage misses a cluster of OpenCPN-parity/strategic items (S-63/CM93, clean-room S-52, GPL-containment interface, internet AIS, serial NMEA as a first-class connectivity item, the plugin probe-contract enforcement). Epic count (24) is above the 12–18 target; SECURE+CONTRACT and CLOUD+NATIVE are the obvious merges. Fix the P0 collisions and encode the shell/style extraction as blocking wave-1 tasks with real downstream deps, and this becomes genuinely parallelizable.
